name: R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route
name_original: Amaya, San Juan Bautista y Peones — circular por Pisuerga
description: Tackle the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route, a moderate 17.2-mile mountain biking trail with 1695 feet of elevation gain in Las Loras UNESCO G
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sotresgudo offers a variety of routes through diverse landscapes. The region is characterized by river valleys, such as the Riomance and Pisuerga, alongside parklands and forested areas. These physical features provide varied terrain suitable for different mountain biking experiences. The trails often follow natural watercourses and traverse open countryside, offering a mix of gradients and surfaces. Best mountain bike trails around Sotresgudo The most popular mountain bike trail is R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic Riomance Valley, offering a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mojón de la Abadesa – Park and Fuente de la Gallina loop from Herrera de Pisuerga, a difficult 32.0 miles (51.6 km) path. This route features varied terrain through parkland and natural springs, providing a demanding ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Lock on the Pisuerga River loop from Herrera de Pisuerga, a 14.9 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Sotresgudo is defined by river valleys, parklands, and open countryside. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Sotresgudo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sotresgudo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Sotresgudo?
A: Sotresgudo offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. Out of 13 available routes, there are 3 easy trails, 8 moderate trails, and 2 difficult trails. This variety ensures options for beginners looking for a gentle ride, as well as experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sotresgudo?
A: Yes, Sotresgudo has several easy mountain bike trails that are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lock on the Pisuerga River loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.9-mile (23.9 km) route that takes about 1 hour 58 minutes, offering a pleasant ride through riverine landscapes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, which is shorter at 9.3 miles (15 km) and takes around 39 minutes.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Trail distances around Sotresgudo vary significantly. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; at 9.3 miles (15 km), taking less than an hour. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cecilia – Aguilar de Campoo Dam loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, stretch over 43.5 miles (70.1 km) and can take more than 6 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Sotresgudo are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20502187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojón de la Abadesa – Park and Fuente de la Gallina loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 32.0-mile (51.6 km) route, and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lock on the Pisuerga River loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Sotresgudo?
A: The terrain around Sotresgudo is quite varied, characterized by river valleys like the Riomance and Pisuerga, alongside parklands and forested areas. You can expect a mix of gradients and surfaces, from smooth paths along watercourses to more rugged sections through open countryside and woodlands.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sotresgudo?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Sotresgudo is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter or icy conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders seeking a challenge will find difficult routes around Sotresgudo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20502187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojón de la Abadesa – Park and Fuente de la Gallina loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 32.0-mile (51.6 km) path with varied terrain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cecilia – Aguilar de Campoo Dam loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 43.5 miles (70.1 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Sotresgudo?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter various natural and historical sights. Notable natural features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4045496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gama Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5831684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Sotresgudo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sotresgudo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, including scenic river valleys and open countryside, and the variety of routes that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or natural monuments to look out for?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural highlights. Keep an eye out for &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt;, a prominent natural monument. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4045496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Amaya&lt;/a&gt; also offer interesting natural formations to explore near some routes.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Sotresgudo area?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails documented around Sotresgudo, offering a good selection for various preferences and skill levels.
tips:
The town of Amaya is one of the few in the area that retains its pre-Romanesque name. Some suspect that the name is derived from Euskera and means the end, and in this case this would allude to its geographical location.

This area has tremendous views. From here you can go up to the Peña Amaya Castle and some caves in the area, but access on two wheels is difficult.
Route through Peña Amaya. There are parrots, canyons, caves, ruinous landscapes, waterfalls... a true spectacle. La Cascada is on a track that goes to the right just before entering the town.
This parish in the town of Amaya, which retains its pre-Roman name, is a Renaissance structure with three naves and cylindrical columns.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is designed to be completed in approximately 2 hours and 33 minutes. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace, skill level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit highlights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route is rated as moderate. It features a mix of terrain with some punchy climbs and descents, making it suitable for riders with some experience. While it offers challenges, it's generally manageable for intermediate mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain on this route. While specific details for this exact trail are limited, similar routes in valley regions often feature a mix of flowy sections, some rocky or technical features, and potentially some narrow, exposed, or overgrown parts. The route includes an elevation gain of over 500 meters, indicating some significant climbs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable highlights. You'll encounter a stunning View of Peña Amaya and Castillo de Peña Amaya, the picturesque Amaya Waterfall, and the historic Parish Church of San Juan Bautista. These spots provide excellent opportunities for breaks and photos.

faqs.question: Does the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. A significant portion (61%) follows the R3 - Valle de Riomance trail. It also includes parts of R1 - Valdeamaya (35%), R2 - Peones de Amaya (34%), and Sendero de Puentes de Amaya (26%), among others. This allows for a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: Is the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this route are not detailed, many mountain biking trails in natural areas allow dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage at the trailhead or the official website for the Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark for the most current pet policies before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail can depend on local climate. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and conditions for mountain biking. Summers in some valley regions can be hot and dry, while winters might bring cooler, tackier conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before planning your trip.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route itself. As part of the Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark, general access to the geopark is typically free, though specific attractions or facilities within it might have their own fees. It's advisable to check the official geopark website for any specific regulations or fees related to certain areas or activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking and trailhead information for the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route is not provided in the available data. However, for routes of this nature, trailheads are often located near villages or points of interest. We recommend checking the komoot tour map for potential starting points and parking areas, or consulting local tourism information for the Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For a moderate mountain bike route like this, it's essential to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and navigation tools (like the komoot app). Depending on the weather, layers of clothing, sunglasses, and sunscreen are also recommended.
